title: Dynamic Web Programming - Quiz 2 (Merged from source documents)
source: Merged from Quiz 2 PDFs and screenshots
deduplicationPolicy: Exact duplicates only (same question text, same option order/text, same correct answer) were removed.

questions.questionMd: What does `response.text()` return?

options.text: A JSON object parsed from the response body
The raw bytes of the response body as a string immediately
A Promise that resolves with the response body as a string
The HTTP status code as a text string

questions.explanationMd: `response.text()` is an asynchronous method that returns a Promise, which resolves with the text representation of the response body.

questions.questionMd: When loading album data from a custom plain-text file, which of the following is a disadvantage mentioned in the lecture?

options.text: Numbers must be manually converted from strings using functions like parseInt()
The file cannot be loaded using the fetch() API
Plain-text files cannot be served over HTTP
Text files do not support arrays of items

questions.explanationMd: In plain-text files, everything is read as a string. Developers must manually parse numbers, dates, or other data types.

questions.questionMd: What does `response.status` contain when used inside a fetch success handler?

options.text: The HTTP status code of the request, such as 200
The text content of the fetched resource
A boolean indicating whether the request succeeded
The name of the file that was fetched

questions.explanationMd: `response.status` holds the numeric HTTP status code returned by the server (e.g., 200 for OK, 404 for Not Found).

questions.questionMd: What does `JSON.parse()` accept as its argument?

options.text: A JavaScript object to be serialized
A Promise returned by fetch()
A fetch response object
A JSON-formatted string

questions.explanationMd: `JSON.parse()` takes a JSON string and transforms it into a JavaScript object.

questions.questionMd: Compared to a custom plain-text format, what is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing
JSON files are always smaller in size than plain-text files
JSON is the only format supported by the fetch() API
JSON files do not require a server to be accessed

questions.explanationMd: JSON is natively supported in JS via `JSON.parse()` and `response.json()`, making deserialization automatic and seamless.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly rewrites `fetch('f.txt').then(onResponse)` using an intermediate variable?

options.text: let p = onResponse; p = fetch('f.txt');
const p = fetch('f.txt'); p.resolve(onResponse);
const p = new Promise('f.txt'); p.then(onResponse);
const p = fetch('f.txt'); p.then(onResponse);

questions.explanationMd: `fetch` returns a Promise, which can be stored in a variable, and then chained with `.then()`.

questions.questionMd: In REST API terminology, what is the "base URL"?

options.text: The fallback URL used when the primary endpoint is unavailable
The root address of the API server to which all endpoint paths are appended
The full URL including all query parameters for a specific request
The URL of the web page that consumes the API

questions.explanationMd: The base URL acts as the consistent starting point for all endpoints in a REST API.

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet respectively?

options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranet), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet
LANs use wireless connections only, while WANs use wired connections only
LANs are always public networks, while WANs are always private networks
LANs operate at the application layer, while WANs operate at the physical layer

questions.explanationMd: LANs are local, private networks (Intranets), and WANs connect them globally to form the broader Internet.

questions.questionMd: What does the `fetch()` function return?

options.text: A Promise
The raw text content of the requested resource
An XMLHttpRequest object
A JSON-parsed JavaScript object

questions.explanationMd: `fetch()` is asynchronous and immediately returns a Promise that resolves to the Response object.

questions.questionMd: What is the fundamental difference between a URL that points to a static file and a URL that represents a REST API endpoint?

options.text: A REST API endpoint returns HTML, while a static file URL always returns JSON
A REST API endpoint causes the server to dynamically generate a response, whereas a static file URL causes the server to return a stored file
A static file URL must include query parameters, while a REST API endpoint never does
A REST API endpoint always requires POST, while static file URLs always use GET

questions.explanationMd: Static files are just retrieved from the disk, while API endpoints execute logic dynamically on the server before responding.

questions.questionMd: Which statement correctly distinguishes the Internet from an Intranet?

options.text: The Internet uses IPv6 only, while an Intranet uses IPv4 only
The Internet is limited to universities, while an Intranet is open to the public
The Internet uses UDP, while an Intranet uses TCP exclusively
The Internet is the global interconnected network, while an Intranet is a private network for a limited group of users

questions.explanationMd: Intranets are private networks, typically used within an organization, whereas the Internet is global and public.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what does it mean to "deserialize" a string?

options.text: To split a string into an array using a separator
To turn a serialized string back into a JavaScript object
To encode a string using HTML entities
To convert a JavaScript object into a JSON string

questions.explanationMd: Deserialization is the process of converting serialized string data (like JSON) back into an in-memory data structure or object.

questions.questionMd: A Promise object can be in one of three states. Which of the following is NOT one of those states?

options.text: Fulfilled
Resolved
Pending
Rejected

questions.explanationMd: The three states of a Promise are Pending, Fulfilled, and Rejected. 'Resolved' describes the outcome, but the strict state name is Fulfilled.

questions.questionMd: When a method is used as a DOM event handler without binding, what does `this` refer to inside that method?

options.text: The global window object
The class instance that owns the method
The HTML element that the event handler was attached to
The event object passed to the handler

questions.explanationMd: In a standard event listener, `this` is automatically bound to the DOM element that fired the event unless bound explicitly or using an arrow function.

questions.questionMd: What is the key difference between `navigator.getUserMedia` and `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia`?

options.text: The deprecated version uses Promises; the newer version uses callbacks
Only the deprecated version supports video streaming
The newer version requires HTTPS while the deprecated version does not
The newer version returns a Promise, the deprecated version uses callbacks

questions.explanationMd: The modern `mediaDevices` API is Promise-based, whereas the old legacy API relied on success and error callback functions.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)?

options.text: It is a company that develops and maintains the Chrome and Firefox web browsers
It is the main international standards organization for the World Wide Web
It is a university consortium that funds research into network security protocols
It is a government agency that registers and manages all domain names globally

questions.explanationMd: The W3C is an international community where member organizations, a full-time staff, and the public work together to develop Web standards.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the `fetch()` function in JavaScript?

options.text: To serialize JavaScript objects into JSON strings
To load resources asynchronously over a network using a Promise-based approach
To synchronously read files from the local file system
To parse HTML documents and manipulate the DOM

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, which company originally developed and used GraphQL before open-sourcing it?

options.text: Netflix
Facebook
GitHub
Spotify

questions.questionMd: How does the lecture describe XMLHttpRequest (XHR) compared to the Fetch API?

options.text: Simpler but less flexible
More secure and widely supported
Faster and more reliable
Clunky and harder to use

questions.questionMd: According to the course evaluation structure, what percentage of the final grade is determined by the midterm component?
Inside an ES6 class constructor, what does the `this` keyword refer to?

options.text: The global window object of the browser
The new object instance being created
The parent class from which the current class inherits
The HTML element that triggered the event

questions.questionMd: Who created JSON according to the lecture?

options.text: Douglas Crockford
Ryan Dahl
Tim Berners-Lee
Brendan Eich

questions.questionMd: By default, without any additional options, which HTTP method does the `fetch()` API use when making a request?

options.text: PUT
POST
DELETE
GET

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the Fetch API in a web browser?

options.text: To manipulate the DOM after a page has loaded
To serialize JavaScript objects into strings
To load external resources such as text and JSON asynchronously
To define callback functions for user interface events

questions.questionMd: Which set of programming languages is associated with front-end web development?

options.text: Node.js, PHP, and Go
SQL, XML, and SGML
Python, Java, and Ruby
HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

questions.questionMd: In a Promise chain such as `fetch(url).then(a).then(b)`, what value does function `b` receive as its argument?

options.text: The original URL string passed to fetch()
The value returned by function a
The raw HTTP response object
The HTTP status code of the response

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly describes the old API that `fetch()` replaced for loading browser resources?

options.text: JSON.parse()
loadFromFile()
navigator.mediaDevices
XMLHttpRequest (XHR)

questions.questionMd: Which of the following statements about `.then()` is correct according to the lecture?

options.text: It can only be called once per Promise object
It replaces the need for the fetch() function
It immediately executes the fetch request synchronously
It lets you attach functions to execute when a Promise succeeds or fails

questions.questionMd: In HTTP communication, what does a status code in the 200 range generally indicate?

options.text: The request was successful
The client must authenticate before proceeding
The server encountered an internal error
The resource was not found

questions.questionMd: Why is storing data as JSON preferable to storing it as plain text when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JSON files load faster because they are compressed by the browser
JSON eliminates the need to manually parse field separators when deserializing structured data
Plain text requires XMLHttpRequest while JSON works with fetch
JSON is the only format the Fetch API is able to read

questions.questionMd: Which method on a fetch `response` object provides built-in support for parsing JSON directly?

options.text: response.json()
response.decode()
response.text()
response.parse()

questions.explanationMd: `response.json()` reads the body and parses it as JSON, returning a Promise.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following statements about `.then()` is correct according to the lecture?

options.text: It lets you attach functions to execute when a Promise succeeds or fails
It replaces the need for the `fetch()` function
It immediately executes the fetch request synchronously
It can only be called once per Promise object

questions.explanationMd: `.then()` attaches fulfillment and optionally rejection handlers to a Promise.

questions.questionMd: The Internet was originally developed in 1969 by which organization to connect computers at research facilities and universities?

options.text: Transmission Control Protocol Institute (TCPI)
European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN)
Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)
World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)

questions.explanationMd: ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet, was funded by DARPA.

questions.questionMd: Why does the lecture suggest extracting the album rendering logic into a separate `_renderAlbums()` helper method?

options.text: Because the `fetch()` API requires a dedicated render callback
To avoid exceeding the maximum allowed number of lines in a single method
To prevent the browser from caching the album images between renders
So the same rendering code can be reused both after the initial fetch and after each sort operation

questions.explanationMd: A helper method avoids duplication by reusing the same rendering logic after loading and sorting.

questions.questionMd: A Promise has a `.then()` method that accepts which of the following?

options.text: A single callback that handles both success and failure identically
A timeout value and a retry count
Functions to execute on success or on error
A URL string and an HTTP method

questions.explanationMd: `.then()` accepts fulfillment and optionally rejection callback functions.

questions.questionMd: In the completed fetch example, which JavaScript method is used to break the fetched text into individual image URLs?

options.text: text.split(' ')
text.replace(' ', ',')
text.slice(' ')
text.join(' ')

questions.explanationMd: `split()` breaks a string into an array using a separator. The screenshot appears to show a space separator.
questions.reviewReason: The separator shown in the screenshot for split(...) is low-resolution and may need visual confirmation.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, the Fetch API is made up of how many functions?

options.text: One
Four
Two
Three

questions.explanationMd: The lecture treats `fetch()` as the single core function of the Fetch API.

questions.questionMd: How are public fields defined on a class instance in ES6?

options.text: By assigning `this.fieldname = value` inside the constructor or a method
By listing them as parameters in the class declaration line
By declaring them with the `public` keyword before the constructor
By using the `var` keyword at the top of the class body

questions.explanationMd: JavaScript class instances commonly receive public fields through assignment to `this`.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes a JavaScript Promise?

options.text: A built in timer that delays code execution by a specified amount
A special array type used to store multiple fetch results
An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation
A synchronous function that immediately returns a value

questions.explanationMd: A Promise represents a future result of an asynchronous operation.

questions.questionMd: On which TCP port does an HTTP server listen by default?
questions.explanationMd: The default port for HTTP is 80.

questions.questionMd: In the completed fetch example, how is each image added to the web page after its URL is extracted?

options.text: The image URL is written directly into the HTML using `innerHTML`
A CSS background property is applied to an existing element
The URL is passed to a separate fetch call to load the image data
A new `` element is created and appended to `document.body`

questions.explanationMd: The example creates an image element and appends it to the DOM.

questions.questionMd: Because functions in JavaScript are objects, which of the following can be called directly on a function variable?

options.text: The `bind()` method, which permanently deletes the function from memory
The `compile()` method, which optimizes the function for performance
The `call()` method, which executes the function's underlying code
The `render()` method, which displays the function's output in the DOM

questions.explanationMd: Functions are callable objects; `call()` is a real method on function objects.

questions.questionMd: How does `response.text()` read the response data according to the lecture?

options.text: Asynchronously, by returning a Promise that resolves with the response string
Synchronously, by returning the string directly
By dispatching a DOM event when reading is complete
By writing the result into a global variable

questions.explanationMd: `response.text()` is asynchronous and returns a Promise resolved with the body text.

questions.questionMd: What problem does calling `this.methodName = this.methodName.bind(this)` in a constructor solve?

options.text: It ensures `this` always refers to the class instance when the method is used as an event handler
It copies the method's code into the HTML element for faster execution
It registers the method as a global function accessible from any scope
It prevents the method from being called more than once per event

questions.explanationMd: Binding fixes the value of `this` so the method still refers to the instance when passed around.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)?

options.text: It is a university consortium that funds research into network security protocols
It is a company that develops and maintains the Chrome and Firefox web browsers
It is a government agency that registers and manages all domain names globally
It is the main international standards organization for the World Wide Web

questions.explanationMd: W3C develops and maintains Web standards.

questions.questionMd: In the discography page example, how does the `SortButton` class communicate a sort action back to the `App` class?

options.text: It directly modifies the `albumInfo` property of the `App` instance
It dispatches a custom keyboard event that triggers the App's event listener
It fires a built-in DOM click event that the `App` class listens to on the document
It calls an `onClickCallback` function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument

questions.explanationMd: The callback-based design lets `SortButton` notify the `App` class without tightly coupling the classes.

questions.questionMd: What does the `fetch()` function return?

options.text: An XMLHttpRequest object
The raw text content of the requested resource
A JSON-parsed JavaScript object
A Promise

questions.explanationMd: `fetch()` immediately returns a Promise for a Response.

questions.questionMd: Compared to a custom plain-text format, what is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JSON is the only format supported by the `fetch()` API
JSON files are always smaller in size than plain-text files
JSON files do not require a server to be accessed
JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing

questions.explanationMd: JSON can be parsed directly into JavaScript objects, unlike ad hoc plain-text formats.

questions.questionMd: When defining methods inside an ES6 class body, which keyword must you omit?

options.text: class
function
const
return

questions.explanationMd: Method definitions in class bodies omit the `function` keyword.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is an important limitation of the CustomEvent API in JavaScript?

options.text: CustomEvents are not supported in any browser that uses the V8 engine
CustomEvents can only be dispatched and listened to on HTML elements, not on arbitrary class instances
CustomEvents can only be created inside ES6 class constructors
CustomEvents cannot carry any additional data in their event object

questions.explanationMd: DOM custom events are dispatched/listened to through event targets such as DOM nodes, not plain class instances by default.

questions.questionMd: What problem does calling `this.methodName = this.methodName.bind(this)` in a constructor solve?

options.text: It prevents the method from being called more than once per event
It ensures `this` always refers to the class instance when the method is used as an event handler
It registers the method as a global function accessible from any scope
It copies the method's code into the HTML element for faster execution

questions.explanationMd: Binding ensures the method keeps the instance context when used as a callback.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what does it mean to "serialize" an object?

options.text: To convert a string into a JavaScript object
To load an object from an external file using fetch
To turn an object into a string that can later be deserialized
To send an object directly to a server over HTTP

questions.explanationMd: Serialization converts structured data into a string form for storage or transfer.

questions.questionMd: In a chained Promise pattern, what must `onResponse` return for the next `.then()` in the chain to receive the text data?

options.text: A resolved string from `response.status`
The Promise returned by `response.text()`
The response object itself
A JSON object created with `JSON.parse()`

questions.explanationMd: Returning `response.text()` passes a Promise into the chain that resolves to the text data.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a characteristic of first-class functions in JavaScript?

options.text: Functions must always be declared before the class definition
Functions cannot be stored in variables or object properties
Functions require the `function` keyword and cannot use arrow syntax
Functions can be passed as parameters to other functions

questions.explanationMd: First-class functions can be stored, passed, and returned like other values.

questions.questionMd: In the discography example, when a method is used as both a fetch callback and an event handler, what must be done in the constructor for `this` to work correctly in both cases?

options.text: Each method must be rebound to the instance using `.bind(this)` and the result stored back on the object
The methods must be declared as arrow functions inside the constructor
Each method must be wrapped in a separate anonymous function before being passed as a callback
The methods must be made static so they do not rely on `this`

questions.explanationMd: Rebinding methods in the constructor keeps `this` correct in both callback contexts.

questions.questionMd: What is the correct syntax for using the real Fetch API with success and error handlers?

options.text: fetch('file.txt').catch(onSuccess).then(onError);
fetch('file.txt', onSuccess, onError);
fetch('file.txt').then(onSuccess, onError);
fetch('file.txt', { success: onSuccess, error: onError });

questions.explanationMd: Promise handlers can be attached with `.then(onSuccess, onError)`.

questions.questionMd: Given the JSON object `{"albums": [{"name": "Daydream", "year": 1995}]}` parsed into a variable called `data`, how would you access the year of the first album?

options.text: data["year"]["albums"][0]
data[0].albums.year
data.albums[0].year
data.albums.year[0]

questions.explanationMd: `albums` is an array property, so you index into it first, then access `year`.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.text: SGML is a standard for specifying markup languages and was used by Tim Berners-Lee to create the HTML specification
SGML is a stylesheet language used to define the visual presentation of XML documents
SGML is a transport protocol that HTTP was built upon
SGML is a scripting language that adds interactivity to static HTML pages

questions.explanationMd: HTML was historically defined as an SGML application.

questions.questionMd: Which command is used to serve local files over HTTP so that `fetch()` can access them during development?

options.text: python3 -m http.server
python3 -m fetch-server
npm start http
node server.js

questions.explanationMd: `python3 -m http.server` starts a simple local HTTP server suitable for development.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, why are web frameworks so commonly used in web development?

options.text: They make software architecture decisions ahead of time and provide starter code, reducing the number of decisions a developer must make
They automatically generate HTML and CSS from back-end database schemas
They compile JavaScript into machine code for faster execution in the browser
They replace the need to learn HTML, CSS, and JavaScript entirely

questions.explanationMd: Frameworks reduce setup effort and architectural decision-making by providing structure and starter code.

questions.questionMd: In JavaScript, what is a closure?

options.text: A function declared inside another function that retains access to the outer function's variables
A method that prevents other code from modifying an object's properties
A special syntax for defining private class fields in ES6
A technique for combining multiple event listeners into a single handler

questions.explanationMd: A closure is a function plus the lexical environment it closes over.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is an important limitation of the CustomEvent API in JavaScript?

options.text: CustomEvents are not supported in any browser that uses the V8 engine
CustomEvents can only be dispatched and listened to on HTML elements, not on arbitrary class instances
CustomEvents cannot carry any additional data in their event object
CustomEvents can only be created inside ES6 class constructors

questions.explanationMd: In the lecture context, CustomEvent was used with DOM event targets rather than arbitrary plain class instances.

questions.questionMd: In the TCP/IP model, what is the responsibility of the Internet Protocol (IP)?

options.text: To convert hostnames into human-readable domain names
To encrypt data before transmission over the network
To route TCP packets to the correct destination address
To guarantee that all packets arrive in the correct order

questions.explanationMd: Among the given options, routing packets toward the destination address is the role closest to IP.
questions.reviewReason: The best answer among the provided options is C, but the option wording says 'TCP packets' rather than the more precise 'IP packets'.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly describes the old API that `fetch()` replaced for loading browser resources?

options.text: XMLHttpRequest (XHR)
JSON.parse()
navigator.mediaDevices
loadFromFile()

questions.explanationMd: Fetch replaced the older XHR pattern for many browser resource requests.

questions.questionMd: Which MIME type indicates that the content being transferred is an HTML web page?

options.text: application/json
text/plain
text/html
image/jpeg

questions.explanationMd: HTML documents are served with the `text/html` MIME type.

questions.questionMd: Inside an ES6 class constructor, what does the `this` keyword refer to?

options.text: The HTML element that triggered the event
The new object instance being created
The parent class from which the current class inherits
The global window object of the browser

questions.explanationMd: Inside a constructor, `this` refers to the instance being initialized.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.text: SGML is a transport protocol that HTTP was built upon
SGML is a standard for specifying markup languages and was used by Tim Berners-Lee to create the HTML specification
SGML is a scripting language that adds interactivity to static HTML pages
SGML is a stylesheet language used to define the visual presentation of XML documents

questions.explanationMd: HTML was originally defined as an SGML application.

questions.questionMd: In the hypothetical asynchronous version of `loadFromFile()` shown in the lecture, how are success and failure handled?

options.text: By checking a return value after the function completes
By wrapping the call in a try-catch block
By polling the function repeatedly until it resolves
By passing `onSuccess` and `onFail` as callback function arguments

questions.explanationMd: The lecture's hypothetical async function uses callbacks for success and failure.

questions.questionMd: When defining methods inside an ES6 class body, which keyword must you omit?

options.text: const
return
function
class

questions.explanationMd: Class method declarations omit the `function` keyword.

questions.questionMd: When passing a callback to `list.findIndex(callback)`, what value should the callback return when the desired element is found?

options.text: The index of the element
null
true
The element itself

questions.explanationMd: `findIndex()` expects the predicate callback to return a truthy value for a match.

questions.questionMd: How does `response.text()` read the response data according to the lecture?

options.text: Synchronously, by returning the string directly
By writing the result into a global variable
Asynchronously, by returning a Promise that resolves with the response string
By dispatching a DOM event when reading is complete

questions.explanationMd: `response.text()` returns a Promise resolved with the text content.

questions.questionMd: Which set of programming languages is associated with front-end web development?

options.text: SQL, XML, and SGML
HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
Node.js, PHP, and Go
Python, Java, and Ruby

questions.explanationMd: The core front-end trio is HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

questions.questionMd: Given the JSON object `{"albums": [{"name": "Daydream", "year": 1995}]}` parsed into a variable called `data`, how would you access the year of the first album?

options.text: data[0].albums.year
data.albums.year[0]
data.albums[0].year
data["year"]["albums"][0]

questions.explanationMd: You access the first element in `albums`, then the `year` property.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a characteristic of first-class functions in JavaScript?

options.text: Functions require the `function` keyword and cannot use arrow syntax
Functions can be passed as parameters to other functions
Functions cannot be stored in variables or object properties
Functions must always be declared before the class definition

questions.explanationMd: First-class functions can be passed around just like any other value.
